---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Cooper Joseph Sharp"
date: "2026-03-26"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}

# ============================================================================
# ICRG: Multinomial Logistic Regression — Paper Analysis
# 0 = Non-Gambler, 1 = Recreational, 2 = Problem Gambler
# Author: Cooper Joseph Sharp
# Date:   2026-03-26
# ============================================================================

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(nnet)
library(broom)
library(car)
library(lmtest)
library(tidycensus)
library(knitr)

# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 1. DATA LOADING & PREPARATION
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

setwd("~/Documents/GitHub/2025-ICRG")
df     <- read.csv("derivatives/scoring/scoring-mastersheet.csv")
df_geo <- read.csv("derivatives/scoring/geo-variables.csv")

# ── Reward sensitivity composite ─────────────────────────────────────────────
df$bisbas_bas <- df$bisbas_drive + df$bisbas_fun_seek + df$bisbas_reward_res

calculate_z <- function(X, X_mean, S) { (X - X_mean) / S }

df$bisbas_bas_z_score <- calculate_z(
  df$bisbas_bas,
  mean(df$bisbas_bas, na.rm = TRUE),
  sd(df$bisbas_bas, na.rm = TRUE)
)
df$spsrq_sr_z_score <- calculate_z(
  df$spsrq_sr,
  mean(df$spsrq_sr, na.rm = TRUE),
  sd(df$spsrq_sr, na.rm = TRUE)
)

df$rs_standard <- df$bisbas_bas_z_score - df$spsrq_sr_z_score

q3 <- quantile(df$rs_standard, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
q1 <- quantile(df$rs_standard, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
iqr <- q3 - q1
df <- df[!(df$rs_standard >= q3 + 1.5 * iqr |
           df$rs_standard <= q1 - 1.5 * iqr) |
           is.na(df$rs_standard), ]

df$rew_composite <- df$spsrq_sr_z_score + df$bisbas_bas_z_score

# ── Gini index by ZIP ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
gini_zip <- get_acs(
  geography = "zcta",
  variables = c(gini = "B19083_001"),
  year = 2021, survey = "acs5"
) %>%
  dplyr::select(GEOID, estimate) %>%
  rename(zipcode = GEOID, gini = estimate)

df <- df %>%
  mutate(zipcode = as.character(demo_zip_prim)) %>%
  left_join(gini_zip, by = "zipcode") %>%
  left_join(df_geo, by = "ResponseId") %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), ~ ifelse(. == -999, NA, .))) %>%
  filter(if_all(c(sogs_total, bbgs_total, mandf_casinog, mandf_onlineg,
                  TotalCrime, unemployed, gini, demo_yrs, demo_gender,
                  rew_composite, susd_depression, susd_mania,
                  scared_total, uclal_total), ~ !is.na(.)))

# ── Classification ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    pg = case_when(
      sogs_total >= 1 & bbgs_total >= 4 & (mandf_casinog + mandf_onlineg) >= 1 ~ 2,
      sogs_total == 0 & bbgs_total <= 3 & (mandf_casinog + mandf_onlineg) == 0 ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ 1
    ),
    income_median_split = factor(
      ifelse(ses_tpi <= 2, "Below Median (<$40k)", "Above Median (≥$40k)"),
      levels = c("Below Median (<$40k)", "Above Median (≥$40k)")
    ),
    race = factor(
      case_when(
        demo_race == 5 ~ "White",
        demo_race == 4 ~ "Black",
        demo_race == 2 ~ "Asian",
        TRUE           ~ "Other"
      ),
      levels = c("White", "Black", "Asian", "Other")
    )
  )

cat("Non-gamblers:", sum(df$pg == 0), "\n")
cat("Recreational:", sum(df$pg == 1), "\n")
cat("Problem:",      sum(df$pg == 2), "\n")


# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 2. ANALYTIC SAMPLE — All three groups
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

dat <- df %>%
  drop_na(
    pg, demo_gender, income_median_split,
    TotalCrime, unemployed, gini, demo_yrs,
    rew_composite,
    dospert_gam, dospert_soc, dospert_inv,
    dospert_hs, dospert_ethic, dospert_rec,
    susd_depression, susd_mania, scared_total,
    uclal_total, bisbas_bas, bisbas_bis, sas_total, planfulness_mean
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    PG = factor(
      case_when(
        pg == 0 ~ "Non-Gambler",
        pg == 1 ~ "Recreational",
        pg == 2 ~ "Problem"
      ),
      levels = c("Non-Gambler", "Recreational", "Problem")
    ),

    gender = factor(demo_gender),
    income = factor(income_median_split),

    z_age           = as.numeric(scale(demo_yrs)),
    z_yoe           = as.numeric(scale(ses_yoe)),
    z_fevs          = as.numeric(scale(fevs_total)),
    z_usidep        = as.numeric(scale(usidep_total)),

    z_reward        = as.numeric(scale(rew_composite)),
    z_dospert_inv   = as.numeric(scale(dospert_inv)),
    z_dospert_gam   = as.numeric(scale(dospert_gam)),
    z_dospert_ethic = as.numeric(scale(dospert_ethic)),
    z_dospert_soc   = as.numeric(scale(dospert_soc)),
    z_dospert_hs    = as.numeric(scale(dospert_hs)),
    z_dospert_rec   = as.numeric(scale(dospert_rec)),

    z_depression    = as.numeric(scale(susd_depression)),
    z_mania         = as.numeric(scale(susd_mania)),
    z_anxiety       = as.numeric(scale(scared_total)),
    z_loneliness    = as.numeric(scale(uclal_total)),
    z_planfulness   = as.numeric(scale(planfulness_mean)),

    z_neigh_disadv = rowMeans(cbind(
      as.numeric(scale(TotalCrime)),
      as.numeric(scale(unemployed)),
      as.numeric(scale(gini))
    ), na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  filter(gender %in% c(1, 2))

dat$gender <- droplevels(dat$gender)
dat$race   <- droplevels(dat$race)

cat("\nAnalytic N =", nrow(dat), "\n")
table(dat$PG, useNA = "ifany")
table(dat$race, dat$PG)


# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 3. MULTINOMIAL LOGISTIC REGRESSION
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Block 1: Demographics + neighbourhood context
mn1 <- multinom(
  PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + income + race + z_yoe,
  data = dat, trace = FALSE
)

# Block 2: + Reward sensitivity & risk-taking
mn2 <- multinom(
  PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + income + race + z_yoe +
    z_reward + z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic +
    z_dospert_gam + z_dospert_inv + z_dospert_rec,
  data = dat, trace = FALSE
)

# Block 3: + Clinical/social symptoms
mn3 <- multinom(
  PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + income + race + z_yoe +
    z_reward + z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic +
    z_dospert_gam + z_dospert_inv + z_dospert_rec +
    z_depression + z_mania + z_anxiety + z_loneliness +
    z_planfulness,
  data = dat, trace = FALSE
)

# Block 4: + Financial vulnerability
mn4 <- multinom(
  PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + income + race + z_yoe +
    z_reward + z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic +
    z_dospert_gam + z_dospert_inv + z_dospert_rec +
    z_depression + z_mania + z_anxiety + z_loneliness +
    z_planfulness +
    z_fevs + z_usidep,
  data = dat, trace = FALSE
)


# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 4. MODEL COMPARISONS
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

cat("\n── LR Tests ──\n")
cat("MN1 vs MN2:\n"); print(anova(mn1, mn2))
cat("MN2 vs MN3:\n"); print(anova(mn2, mn3))
cat("MN3 vs MN4:\n"); print(anova(mn3, mn4))

data.frame(
  Model    = c("MN1: Context", "MN2: + Reward/Risk",
               "MN3: + Clinical", "MN4: + Financial"),
  AIC      = round(c(AIC(mn1), AIC(mn2), AIC(mn3), AIC(mn4)), 1),
  Deviance = round(c(deviance(mn1), deviance(mn2), deviance(mn3), deviance(mn4)), 1)
) %>% kable()


# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 5. RELATIVE RISK RATIOS
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

get_rrr_table <- function(model) {
  coefs <- summary(model)$coefficients
  ses   <- summary(model)$standard.errors
  z_vals <- coefs / ses
  p_vals <- 2 * pnorm(-abs(z_vals))

  outcomes <- rownames(coefs)
  terms    <- colnames(coefs)

  do.call(rbind, lapply(outcomes, function(outcome) {
    data.frame(
      contrast = outcome,
      term     = terms,
      RRR      = round(exp(coefs[outcome, ]), 3),
      z        = round(z_vals[outcome, ], 3),
      p.value  = round(p_vals[outcome, ], 3),
      sig = case_when(
        p_vals[outcome, ] < .001 ~ "***",
        p_vals[outcome, ] < .01  ~ "**",
        p_vals[outcome, ] < .05  ~ "*",
        p_vals[outcome, ] < .1   ~ ".",
        TRUE                     ~ ""
      ),
      row.names = NULL
    )
  }))
}

rrr_mn4 <- get_rrr_table(mn4)

cat("\n── Recreational vs. Non-Gambler ──\n")
rrr_mn4 %>% filter(contrast == "Recreational") %>% kable()

cat("\n── Problem vs. Non-Gambler ──\n")
rrr_mn4 %>% filter(contrast == "Problem") %>% kable()


# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 6. COEFFICIENT COMPARISON PLOT
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

col_rec  <- "purple1"
col_prob <- "purple4"

coefs <- summary(mn4)$coefficients
ses   <- summary(mn4)$standard.errors

plot_dat <- do.call(rbind, lapply(rownames(coefs), function(outcome) {
  data.frame(
    contrast = outcome,
    term     = colnames(coefs),
    RRR      = exp(coefs[outcome, ]),
    CI_low   = exp(coefs[outcome, ] - 1.96 * ses[outcome, ]),
    CI_high  = exp(coefs[outcome, ] + 1.96 * ses[outcome, ]),
    row.names = NULL
  )
})) %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)")

ggplot(plot_dat, aes(x = RRR, y = reorder(term, RRR), colour = contrast)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
  geom_errorbarh(
    aes(xmin = CI_low, xmax = CI_high),
    height = 0.2, position = position_dodge(width = 0.6)
  ) +
  geom_point(size = 3, position = position_dodge(width = 0.6)) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c("Recreational" = col_rec, "Problem" = col_prob),
    labels = c("Recreational vs. NG", "Problem vs. NG")
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Relative Risk Ratio (log scale)", y = NULL,
    colour = "Contrast",
    title = "Multinomial Model (MN4): Both Contrasts vs. Non-Gambler"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")


# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 7. PREDICTED PROBABILITIES
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

pred_grid <- expand.grid(
  z_neigh_disadv  = 0,
  gender          = levels(dat$gender)[1],
  income          = levels(dat$income)[1],
  z_age           = 0,
  race            = levels(dat$race)[1],
  z_planfulness   = 0,
  z_usidep        = 0,
  z_fevs          = 0,
  z_yoe           = 0,
  z_reward        = seq(-2, 2, length.out = 100),
  z_dospert_inv   = 0, z_dospert_gam = 0, z_dospert_ethic = 0,
  z_dospert_soc   = 0, z_dospert_hs  = 0, z_dospert_rec   = 0,
  z_depression    = 0, z_mania = 0, z_anxiety = 0,
  z_loneliness    = 0
)

pred_probs <- predict(mn4, newdata = pred_grid, type = "probs")
pred_grid <- cbind(pred_grid, pred_probs)

pred_long <- pred_grid %>%
  dplyr::select(z_reward, `Non-Gambler`, Recreational, Problem) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(`Non-Gambler`, Recreational, Problem),
    names_to  = "Group",
    values_to = "Probability"
  ) %>%
  mutate(Group = factor(Group, levels = c("Non-Gambler", "Recreational", "Problem")))

label_dat <- pred_long %>%
  group_by(Group) %>%
  filter(z_reward == max(z_reward))

ggplot(pred_long, aes(z_reward, Probability, colour = Group, fill = Group)) +
  geom_area(alpha = 0.08, position = "identity", linewidth = 0) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.4) +
  geom_label(
    data = label_dat,
    aes(label = Group),
    hjust = 1, nudge_x = -0.05,
    size = 3.8, fontface = "bold",
    label.padding = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    label.size = 0, fill = "white", alpha = 0.85
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-1, 0, 1),
             linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey70", linewidth = 0.4) +
  annotate("text", x = c(-1, 0, 1), y = 0.98,
           label = c("\u22121 SD", "Mean", "+1 SD"),
           size = 3, colour = "grey50", vjust = 1) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::percent,
    limits = c(0, 1),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(-2, 2, by = 0.5),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.02))
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c(
    "Non-Gambler"  = "#B85C8A",
    "Recreational" = "#8B5CF6",
    "Problem"      = "#3B0764"
  )) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Non-Gambler"  = "#B85C8A",
    "Recreational" = "#8B5CF6",
    "Problem"      = "#3B0764"
  )) +
  labs(
    x = "\nReward Sensitivity (z-score)",
    y = "Predicted Probability\n",
    title = "Predicted Group Membership by Reward Sensitivity",
    subtitle = "Multinomial model (MN4) \u2014 all other predictors held at sample mean",
    caption = "Reference: White male, above-median income"
  ) +
  guides(colour = "none", fill = "none") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, margin = margin(b = 4)),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(colour = "grey40", size = 11, margin = margin(b = 12)),
    plot.caption     = element_text(colour = "grey50", size = 9, hjust = 0),
    axis.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    axis.text        = element_text(size = 11),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "grey92"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.margin      = margin(20, 20, 15, 15)
  )

# ── HC vs. All Gamblers (collapsed) ──────────────────────────────────────────
reward_seq <- seq(-2, 2, length.out = 100)

avg_probs <- do.call(rbind, lapply(reward_seq, function(r) {
  tmp <- dat
  tmp$z_reward <- r
  preds <- predict(mn4, newdata = tmp, type = "probs")
  data.frame(
    z_reward    = r,
    Non_Gambler = mean(preds[, "Non-Gambler"]),
    Gambler     = mean(preds[, "Recreational"] + preds[, "Problem"])
  )
}))

pred_binary <- avg_probs %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(Non_Gambler, Gambler),
    names_to  = "Group",
    values_to = "Probability"
  ) %>%
  mutate(Group = factor(
    ifelse(Group == "Non_Gambler", "Non-Gambler", "Gambler"),
    levels = c("Non-Gambler", "Gambler")
  ))
ggplot(pred_binary, aes(z_reward, Probability, colour = Group, fill = Group)) +
  geom_area(alpha = 0.08, position = "identity", linewidth = 0) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.4) +
  geom_label(
    data = label_dat_bin,
    aes(label = Group),
    hjust = 1, nudge_x = -0.05,
    size = 3.8, fontface = "bold",
    label.padding = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    label.size = 0, fill = "white", alpha = 0.85
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-1, 0, 1),
             linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey70", linewidth = 0.4) +
  annotate("text", x = c(-1, 0, 1), y = 0.98,
           label = c("\u22121 SD", "Mean", "+1 SD"),
           size = 3, colour = "grey50", vjust = 1) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::percent,
    limits = c(0, 1),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(-2, 2, by = 0.5),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.02))
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c(
    "Non-Gambler" = "#B85C8A",
    "Gambler"     = "#3B0764"
  )) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Non-Gambler" = "#B85C8A",
    "Gambler"     = "#3B0764"
  )) +
  labs(
    x = "\nReward Sensitivity (z-score)",
    y = "Predicted Probability\n",
    title = "Non-Gambler vs. All Gamblers by Reward Sensitivity",
    subtitle = "Averaged over all sample covariates \u2014 derived from multinomial model (MN4)"
    ) +
  guides(colour = "none", fill = "none") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, margin = margin(b = 4)),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(colour = "grey40", size = 11, margin = margin(b = 12)),
    plot.caption     = element_text(colour = "grey50", size = 9, hjust = 0),
    axis.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    axis.text        = element_text(size = 11),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "grey92"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.margin      = margin(20, 20, 15, 15)
  )


# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 8. CLASSIFICATION ACCURACY
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

predicted_class <- predict(mn4, type = "class")

cat("\n── Confusion Matrix (MN4) ──\n")
print(table(Predicted = predicted_class, Actual = dat$PG))

cat("\nOverall Accuracy:",
    round(mean(predicted_class == dat$PG, na.rm = TRUE), 3), "\n")

cat("\nPer-class sensitivity:\n")
for (lvl in levels(dat$PG)) {
  actual_lvl    <- dat$PG == lvl
  predicted_lvl <- predicted_class == lvl
  sensitivity   <- sum(actual_lvl & predicted_lvl) / sum(actual_lvl)
  cat(sprintf("  %s: %.1f%%\n", lvl, sensitivity * 100))
}

```
